# Going Digital: How Legal Technology Is Transforming Law Firms

## Image with digital transformation logos

![Image with digital transformation logos]()

## What does “digital transformation” mean?

The term “digital transformation” refers to integrating technology into all business areas. Technological advancements—such as artificial intelligence, automation, CRMs, and cloud-based systems—have reshaped the legal industry.

Adopting these technologies improves efficiency and reduces costs, so you can set up a law firm that works for you instead of the other way around.

## How can my law firm stay relevant?

### Embrace innovative tools

First and foremost, don't fight change; embrace it. Many lawyers were initially hesitant to adopt legal technology. They had systems and were skeptical about jumping on the digital bandwagon.

Recently, it has become the industry standard to use CRMs, automation, and social media as part of the day-to-day operations of a law firm.

If you've struggled to embrace change because you were unsure where to start, investing in the right software is an excellent first step. There are plenty of tools available to support lawyers in growing their firms.

What's most important is to find the right ones that will help you achieve the goals you have in mind for your practice.

### Make a great first impression

Your website and social media are the first way visitors can tell who you are and what you can do for them. Making a great first impression is essential to converting website traffic to clients.

Throughout this article, we will dive into tools and strategies to assist you in making an outstanding first impression online.

### Be active on social media

Social media is great for networking and marketing. It allows lawyers to connect with other professionals, build relationships, and gain referrals.

It's also a free marketing tool. By consistently posting valuable content, you'll be at the forefront of your followers’ minds if they need a lawyer.

For example, [Money Lawyer Erika](https://www.tiktok.com/@erikakullberg?lang=en) on TikTok has gained 9 million followers by sharing legal tips. You don't need to go viral for social media to make an impact. It's all about building connections and trust with your audience, regardless of size.

### Respond quickly

Response time matters because it indicates a firm’s ability to handle client inquiries and concerns. A quick reply builds confidence because it shows the law firm is attentive.

If you don’t respond in 24 hours, you’ve failed to meet the needs of 87% of people. Showing up online gets leads in the door. But if your response time lags, the conversion rate plummets. Don’t waste your resources; put systems in place to improve responsiveness.

Intaker’s chat sends an automated follow-up message once a lead fills out their information. During the lead-nurturing phase, messaging [sequences](https://intaker.com/home/products/workflow-sequences.html) can be used to automate the follow-up process.

Lastly, you can create an arsenal of [text templates](https://blog.intaker.com/post/16-text-templates-to-connect-with-clients-and-leads) for any commonly asked questions to save your team time when responding to clients and leads.

The first step is to audit the current state of your law firm. Look at the technology, processes, and workflows currently being used and identify areas for improvement. Next, develop a plan with specific goals. Invest in tools and services to meet these objectives, and measure the effectiveness of the efforts over time.

SUMMARY:

FULL CONTENT:
As we step further into the digital age, the legal industry is undergoing a significant transformation. An increasing number of law firms and legal departments are recognizing the potential of legal technology in streamlining operations, improving efficiency, and delivering superior client service. Several exciting technological advancements expected to revolutionize the legal landscape in 2024.

Legal technology, or 'legal tech,' refers to the use of technology and software to provide legal services or improve efficiencies as legal organizations. It is a rapidly evolving field that covers a wide spectrum of applications, ranging from document automation and contract analysis to legal research and electronic discovery. Legal tech aims to simplify and modernize legal processes, helping individuals and businesses navigate the complex legal system more efficiently.

In this blog post, we will delve into some of the top legal technology trends to watch for. These trends reflect the ongoing digital transformation in the legal sector and highlight the innovative ways in which technology is being utilized to redefine the practice of law. 

## 1. The Rise of the AI Lawyer

Artificial intelligence (AI) has been a hot topic in the legal industry for quite some time now. With its ability to analyze vast amounts of data, identify patterns and make predictions, AI technology is transforming the way lawyers work. We can expect to see even more adoption of AI-powered tools in the legal field.

One area where AI is making significant strides is in legal research. Gone are the days when lawyers had to manually sift through piles of case law and statutes. AI-powered tools such as [ROSS Intelligence](https://www.rossintelligence.com/features) and [Lex Machina](https://lexmachina.com/) are now enabling legal professionals to conduct faster, more thorough research.

Another way AI will be used is for for legal document drafting. Lawyers can begin drafting and brainstorming legal documents quickly and efficiently by using AI tools. It does, however, remain imperative that lawyers and legal professionals thoroughly review and edit the computer-generated content to ensure accuracy and maintain the desired tone.

These tools use natural language processing (NLP) algorithms to understand complex legal concepts and generate relevant insights, saving lawyers valuable time and allowing them to focus on higher-value tasks.

## 2. Cybersecurity Is Paramount

As we continue to witness the rapid growth of legal technology, it becomes increasingly important to address the issue of cybersecurity. As more law firms and legal departments rely on advanced technological tools, the need for robust cybersecurity measures becomes paramount.

With the rise of the AI lawyer and the increasing use of cloud-based platforms, protecting sensitive client data from potential cyber threats is crucial. The legal industry must prioritize cybersecurity to ensure the confidentiality and integrity of client information, maintaining the trust and confidence of their clients.

In the digital age, the legal sector faces numerous cybersecurity challenges. Hackers and cybercriminals are constantly finding new ways to exploit vulnerabilities in software and networks, posing a significant risk to legal organizations. Legal technology trends will undoubtedly include enhanced security measures such as multi-factor authentication, encryption, and advanced threat detection systems. Law firms and legal departments must invest in robust cybersecurity infrastructure and stay up to date with the latest industry standards to protect their clients' valuable information.

Furthermore, with the increasing adoption of cloud-based platforms for document management and collaboration, the importance of cybersecurity becomes even more critical. Law firms and legal departments must carefully select reputable cloud service providers that prioritize security and compliance. Implementing secure file transfer protocols, regular data backups, and stringent access controls are essential in safeguarding sensitive legal information.

By making cybersecurity a top priority, legal organizations can confidently embrace the benefits and efficiencies provided by legal tech while ensuring the confidentiality and privacy of their clients' data.

## 3. Automated CLE, MCLE and Legal Certificates

Another trend that has gained significant traction is the seamless delivery and automation of Continuing Legal Education (CLE), Mandatory Continuing Legal Education (MCLE), and legal certificates to lawyers. This process is becoming much more streamlined and efficient.

Organizations like [BeaconLive](/legal) have enabled law firms and legal organizations to easily create a comprehensive catalog of their accredited legal content, conduct webinars and online CLE sessions, and streamline the delivery and tracking of legal certificates. This automation not only eliminates countless hours of administrative tasks but also ensures a more efficient and hassle-free experience for both the providers and recipients of legal education.

These platforms allow legal professionals to [complete their CLE and MCLE courses online, at their own pace, and in a more cost-effective manner](/blog/what-is-an-lms-for-law-firms-beaconlive). These platforms provide access to a wide range of courses, covering various legal topics, and offer the convenience of tracking and managing certificates electronically. By automating CLE, MCLE, and legal certificates, legal professionals can save time, stay up to date with the latest legal developments, and ensure compliance with their jurisdiction's requirements.

With the traditional complexities and time-consuming processes associated with CLE and certificate delivery becoming a thing of the past, legal organizations can now shift their focus to other critical aspects of their practice. By leveraging the power of technology, law firms can maximize productivity, enhance client services and allocate resources effectively.

## 4. Disruptions In Virtual Contract Signing

One of the most significant advancements in the legal technology landscape in recent years is the rise of electronic signature platforms. Among these platforms, [DocuSign](https://www.docusign.com/solutions/departments/legal) has emerged as a game-changer, revolutionizing the way contracts are signed and executed. We can expect DocuSign to continue disrupting the traditional contract signing process and becoming even more prevalent in the legal industry.

Gone are the days of printing out contracts, signing them by hand, and mailing them back and forth. With DocuSign, legal professionals can now digitally sign contracts with just a few clicks, eliminating the need for physical paperwork and streamlining the entire signing process. This technology not only saves time and resources but also enhances efficiency and reduces the risk of errors or delays.

DocuSign's user-friendly interface makes it easy for parties to review and sign contracts electronically, from anywhere in the world. Whether it's a simple agreement or a complex legal document, DocuSign provides a secure and legally binding way to execute contracts digitally. The platform ensures the integrity of the document by encrypting it and capturing a comprehensive audit trail, providing a reliable record of the signing process.

DocuSign also allows parties to add comments, make annotations, and request signatures from multiple signatories. These collaborative features streamline the negotiation and approval process, enabling parties to work together seamlessly, even when they are geographically dispersed.

The widespread adoption of DocuSign has not only transformed the way contracts are signed but has also had a significant impact on the legal profession as a whole. Legal organizations that embrace this technology can reduce administrative burdens, increase productivity, and deliver faster results to clients. Moreover, DocuSign's integration capabilities with other legal tech tools, such as contract management systems and document automation software, further enhance its value and versatility.

## 4. Blockchain Beyond Bitcoin

Blockchain technology will continue to transform the legal industry, offering new ways to handle contracts and agreements. This decentralized and transparent ledger system has the potential to transform how legal transactions are conducted, making them more secure, efficient, and cost-effective. By leveraging blockchain technology, legal professionals can streamline processes such as contract management, property transactions, and intellectual property rights.

The immutability and transparency of the blockchain ensure that all transactions are recorded and stored securely, reducing the risk of fraud or tampering. Additionally, smart contracts, which are self-executing agreements stored on the blockchain, can automate various legal processes, removing the need for intermediaries and reducing the potential for human error.

As blockchain technology continues to evolve, it is becoming increasingly clear that it will be a trend to watch out for. The potential applications of blockchain in the legal industry are vast and diverse. For example, blockchain can be used to create digital identities for individuals and organizations, allowing for secure and efficient verification processes. This can streamline client onboarding and KYC (Know Your Customer) procedures for law firms and legal departments. Additionally, blockchain can enhance the security and efficiency of legal document management systems by providing a tamper-proof and auditable record of all changes and transactions.

Furthermore, blockchain technology can help address some of the challenges associated with cross-border transactions and international legal disputes. By utilizing blockchain, legal professionals can create smart contracts that automatically execute and enforce agreements, reducing the need for traditional legal processes and lengthy court proceedings. This can greatly simplify and expedite international transactions, ensuring that all parties involved adhere to their contractual obligations.

Overall, the blockchain has the potential to transform the legal industry, making it more secure, efficient, and accessible for all stakeholders involved.

## 5. Consolidation of Technology Vendors

In addition to the advancements in cybersecurity, automated legal education, and virtual contract signing, another significant trend that will shape the legal technology landscape is the consolidation of technology vendors. As the legal industry continues to embrace digital transformation, there is a growing need for streamlined and integrated solutions that can meet multiple technological requirements.

Currently, many law firms and legal departments rely on multiple technology vendors to fulfill various needs, such as document management, case management, timekeeping, billing, and more. However, managing and integrating these disparate systems can be complex and time-consuming. It often requires extensive training and support to ensure smooth operations and seamless data flow between different platforms.

Recognizing this challenge, the legal technology market is witnessing a shift towards consolidation. We can expect to see more comprehensive and all-in-one solutions emerging, offering a wide range of functionalities within a single platform. These integrated platforms will not only simplify technology management but also enhance efficiency and productivity for legal professionals.

By consolidating technology vendors, law firms and legal departments can benefit from a more streamlined and cohesive workflow. They can avoid the hassle of managing multiple systems, reduce the risk of data inconsistencies, and improve collaboration among different teams. With a unified platform, legal professionals can access all the necessary tools and information they need from a single interface, eliminating the need to switch between various applications.

Furthermore, consolidating technology vendors can also lead to cost savings and improved vendor management. Instead of dealing with multiple contracts, licenses, and support agreements, legal organizations can negotiate and manage a single agreement with an integrated solution provider. This not only simplifies the procurement process but also allows for better negotiation power and potential cost reductions.

Additionally, consolidating technology vendors can also improve data security and compliance. With a single platform, legal organizations can implement consistent security measures and ensure compliance with industry standards and regulations. They can more effectively manage user access controls, monitor data usage, and implement data retention policies.

However, it is important for legal organizations to carefully evaluate and select the right integrated solution that best fits their specific needs. They should consider factors such as scalability, customization options, user-friendliness, and compatibility with existing systems. It is crucial to assess the track record and reputation of the technology vendor, ensuring their commitment to security, support, and ongoing development.

In conclusion, the consolidation of technology vendors is a trend that will reshape the legal technology landscape. By embracing integrated platforms, law firms and legal departments can simplify technology management, enhance productivity, and improve data security. With comprehensive solutions that offer a wide range of functionalities, legal professionals can focus more on delivering high-quality legal services and less on dealing with the complexities of multiple systems. The consolidation of technology vendors is a step towards a more efficient and interconnected legal industry.

## Conclusion

In conclusion, the legal sector is rapidly evolving with the influx of emerging technology trends. From automation and AI integration to enhanced cybersecurity and the revolutionary impact of blockchain, these advancements are set to redefine how legal work is conducted and services delivered.

It's pivotal for law firms, legal professionals, and stakeholders to stay abreast of these trends to leverage their potential fully. By doing so, they can streamline processes, ensure greater accuracy, enhance client service, and ultimately, [stay ahead in the competitive legal landscape](/blog/what-it-takes-to-keep-your-cle-program-competitive-in-an-evolving-market). The future of legal technology is indeed exciting and holds limitless possibilities.

#### Free Guide

The Small Firm’s Guide to Big Tech
----------------------------------

While any change in how you operate your law office can feel overwhelming, there’s never been a better time to start migrating your office structures online. Big tech changed the game in how we run our everyday lives, so stay up to date with the latest available software solutions to keep clients happy.

[Get free e-book](https://www.rocketmatter.com/guides/the-small-firms-guide-to-big-tech/?channel_type1=Guide&campaign1=blog-syndication-exit-intent-the-small-firms-guide-to-big-tech&content_topic=Law-Accounting%20Office%20Management)

The digital age has changed our collective relationship to communication — how we receive, process, and express information is now almost boundless and instantaneous. The legal industry is not exempt from this, most notably with how clients expect to be served.

Today, better and expedited access to legal services is simply a baseline to meet, not a privilege. But if this is the case for what clients require, then why are there still too many law firms buried in the unnecessary abundance of physical paperwork?

The answer is simple: a lack of digital transformation in law firms. While there’s a semblance of comfort in sticking to familiar yet largely outdated tools, they are also a great detriment to legal practitioners.

With client expectations at a steady rise, it’s clear that the legal industry is reaching its tipping point — and the only way to regain balance is to implement digital transformation in law firms’ day-to-day systems.

What Is Legal Digital Transformation?
-------------------------------------

Imagine AI-powered legal research that takes minutes instead of hours, automated document drafting that eliminates errors, or cloud-based case management that lets your team collaborate from anywhere. This is what you can expect when law firms undergo a digital transformation — you unlock a means to work smarter, not harder.

The concept simply means that law firms must adopt the latest technologies to optimize their operations and improve efficiency, ideally touching on every aspect of legal work — from case management to client communication.

Key Benefits of Digital Transformation to Your Law Firm Growth
--------------------------------------------------------------

Why do we need digital transformation in the legal industry? Practitioners themselves — a whopping [91% of them](https://www.law.com/legaltechnews/2023/11/08/lawyers-main-barriers-with-tech-adoption-organizational-challenges-lack-of-skills-and-fear/?slreturn=2025020352802) — believe it’s a crucial step. Law firms are under constant pressure to manage the administrative work that comes with increasing caseloads, all while maintaining high-quality service to their clients.

Here’s how legal digital transformation helps firms fare better while scaling their practice.

### Enhanced Efficiency and Performance

Adopting the latest legal technology lets law firms automate repetitive tasks like contract review, document drafting, and case analysis, among others. This gives you the much-needed leeway to allocate your resources more strategically, focus on higher-value work, and ultimately build a stronger bottom line. Digital law firm tools make processes more systematic, competitive, and productive, rendering your work less prone to human errors and inaccuracies.

For example, streamlining your workflows through AI-powered tools can save you around seven hours, boosting your productivity [by 20 to 30%](https://www.ft.com/content/8dbeecee-c242-4b22-987b-96b492f27d1e). VWV, a mid-sized law firm in the United Kingdom, has even reported an [80% improvement in its contract review time](https://www.thetimes.com/business-money/entrepreneurs/article/the-secret-to-making-ai-work-is-to-get-people-trained-and-enthused-enterprise-network-dmwfnfqzn), thanks to AI use.

### Higher Client Engagement

This transformation in the legal industry also directly addresses what today’s clients expect from law firms, which is to receive the same level of digital convenience they get from other service providers.

In fact, two-thirds of clients expect [personalized and tech-enabled legal services](https://www.salesforce.com/resources/research-reports/state-of-the-connected-customer/), according to a survey by Salesforce. However, Ultimedia’s 022/23 Digital Maturity Report found that only 3% of law firms offer it — take this as an opportunity to differentiate your firm from others.

### Strict Cybersecurity Compliance

With more sophisticated cyber threats looming on the horizon and targeting law firms specifically, security has become a must that cannot be sidelined. Digital transformation in law firms allows for better cybersecurity measures, compliance, and data protection. It enables firms to meet ethical and legal standards, which is all the more important in 2025, as 97% of legal departments are expected to [require firms to disclose the technology stack they use](https://images.go.wolterskluwer.com/Web/WoltersKluwer/%7Bf38293b3-9969-4fd4-8fc6-daea89537ff1%7D_LR_white_paper_2022_09-01_FINAL_single.pdf) before making hiring decisions.

## [The Whatfix Blog | Drive Digital Adoption](/blog/)

# Digital Transformation of Law Firms (+Examples)

Law firms face pressure to modernize tools and processes or avoid falling behind client demands and competitor capabilities. And lawyers recognize this, with [91%](https://www.law.com/legaltechnews/2023/11/08/lawyers-main-barriers-with-tech-adoption-organizational-challenges-lack-of-skills-and-fear/) saying its critical for their firm to adopt the latest legal software and technology.

But while more than [half](https://ultimedia.agency/digital-transformation-in-law-firms/) of the top law firms globally increased their IT and technology spending in 2022, all top 100 firms are considered digitalization laggards compared to similar-sized enterprises in other industries.

However, this shift isn’t just about buying the latest technology. Real [digital transformation](https://whatfix.com/digital-transformation/) in the legal sector changes how legal professionals provide value, operate their businesses, and provide better client experiences.

In this article, we’ll explore the impacts of digitization on legal practices. First, we’ll examine how leading firms use legal technology to enhance productivity, insights, and experiences. We’ll also discuss common roadblocks to digital adoption and how to overcome them. Plus, we’ll showcase real-world examples of digital transformation elevating legal work.

## What Is Digital Transformation in the Legal Industry?

Digital transformation revolutionizes how legal services are delivered and experienced through technology integration. This looks like using data, analytics, and automation to operate smarter. This shift affects all aspects of the way firms run. It also empowers legal professionals to meet client demands for convenient, transparent, and personalized interactions.

According to research from [Thomson Reuters](https://www.thomsonreuters.com/en-us/posts/technology/digital-transformation-report-2023/), more law firms are beginning to combine their digital transformation projects into an overarching strategy that has buy-in from the C-suite level. 83% say that digital transformation is central to their strategy.

Transformation requires reimagining existing workflows, processes, and business models by embedding new tools into everyday operations. This can span across the entire legal lifecycle. From first consultations to case management and virtual court appearances, technology removes geographic barriers and scheduling limitations.

It also streamlines internal efforts with intelligent automation and enhances security protocols. As a whole, digitization leads to improved efficiency, accuracy, and collaboration both externally and internally.

## Benefits of Law Firm Digitalization

If you’re unsure what your digital investment will provide for your firm, it can be difficult to justify a significant investment and [organizational change](https://whatfix.com/blog/types-of-organizational-change/). Look at how legal professionals and clients benefit from digitization initiatives.

### 1. Reduced human errors

Legacy processes that rely on manual data entry, document review, and case management are vulnerable to mistakes. This is a problem in a high-stakes industry where errors can negatively impact legal outcomes. Modern tools like [smart contracts](https://whatfix.com/blog/smart-contracts-changing-legal-landscape/), automated filings, and AI-powered legal research engines boost accuracy across the board.

With algorithms self-correcting errors in real-time, there’s a safety net guarding against the consequences of these mistakes. This shift creates more reliable client experiences. Precision and accuracy are now easier to achieve, which boosts quality standards.

### 2. Increased operational efficiency

New enterprise software solutions like case management software, document automation, eDiscovery platforms, and cloud-based collaboration cut the time and resources spent on administration. Legal professionals can instantly access files from any location or use templates to focus on what they do best.

Efficiency enhancements like this also lead to faster responses to client requests. Employees can find the details to answer questions quickly and track down documents they may need to move a case forward.

### 3. Better customer experience

Online portals, mobile apps, personalization, and AI chatbots allow 24/7 case access and communication, bringing legal resources directly to clients for self-service convenience. This is important at a time when most people expect the assistance they need to be a click away.

In fact, [80%](https://www.clio.com/blog/law-firm-digital-transformation-report/) of people surveyed said they would be more likely to do business with a law firm that provided a personalized content experience – but only 3% of law firms analyzed showed signs of personalization on their websites.

Digital tools also make personalization possible. Law firms can use data to anticipate their client’s needs or send marketing messages tailored to current events and preferences.

### 4. Improved knowledge management

The average employee spends [3.6 hours per day](https://venturebeat.com/business/report-employees-spend-3-6-hours-each-day-searching-for-info-increasing-burnout/) searching for information. Sophisticated AI search engines and digital databases can provide streamlined access to volumes of case law, precedents, and statutes – all in your legal employees’ workflows. This helps staff stay on top of the developments they need to provide great service.

Improved [knowledge management](https://whatfix.com/blog/knowledge-management/) also increases productivity for all kinds of internal processes, as employees don’t have to waste valuable time tracking down firm information.

## Digital Transformation Challenges for Law Firms

Digitalization has endless potential, but the road to [legacy application modernization](https://whatfix.com/blog/legacy-application-modernization/) has hurdles. Here are some pitfalls you should know when taking on digital transformation and how to overcome them.

### 1. Regulatory compliance and privacy concerns

Guarding access to sensitive client data is non-negotiable, but digital systems introduce vulnerabilities. Regulatory requirement violations come with heavy fines and reputation damage, so investing in security is important.

However, according to an ILTA survey in 2022, only 22% of firms are running weekly vulnerability scans. While up from 16% in 2020, this still highlights a huge security vulnerability in the legal sector.

Access controls, encryption, and auditing procedures work together to keep data safe. As you implement these safeguards, ensure everyone on your team knows how to engage with them. This is critical, as [human error](https://www.comparitech.com/blog/information-security/human-error-cybersecurity-stats/) is the leading cause of cybersecurity incidents that put information at risk.

### 2. Internal resistance to change

Internal [resistance to change](https://whatfix.com/blog/causes-of-resistance-to-change/) from those who are used to existing processes hinders [end-user adoption](https://whatfix.com/blog/user-adoption-strategy/) and impacts ROI for law firm digitalization projects. Leadership must focus on securing buy-in from leaders, lawyers, administrators, and more across their firm.

There are several ways to approach this. For example, ask for input when choosing new [change management tools](https://whatfix.com/blog/change-management-tools/) and involve all stakeholders in technology integration planning. Then ensure everyone has the training and support they need to make a smooth transition. This helps create a culture of positive change.

### 3. Updating legacy systems and technology

44% of lawyers said that the biggest barrier to tech investment was organizational, due to older, outdated, manual processes and an overall lack of [digital maturity](https://whatfix.com/blog/digital-maturity/).

[Retiring legacy systems](https://whatfix.com/blog/legacy-application-modernization/) and moving to modern platforms brings the potential for disruption. It’s important to focus on one system or data source at a time to move digitization forward while minimizing downtime.

[IT modernization](https://whatfix.com/blog/legacy-application-modernization/) requires planning. Staff leading the change must understand how current systems work and what they’re connected to – from there, it’s possible to prioritize workflows that are easier to transition. To take it a step further, hire an external [digital transformation consultant](https://whatfix.com/blog/digital-transformation-consulting/) to oversee this process and make IT modernization smoother.

### 4. Contextual end-user onboarding and training

36% of lawyers and legal company leaders said that a lack of tech skills and the need for [digital upskilling](https://whatfix.com/blog/upskilling-your-workforce/) is the biggest hurdle to tech adoption of digital investments. At the same time, 92% of lawyers said that appropriate [software training](https://whatfix.com/blog/train-employees-on-new-software/) is important to them.

Firms must set staff up for [digital adoption](https://whatfix.com/digital-adoption/) success through role-specific guidance, not one-size-fits-all tutorials. Think through how new tools will fit into the legal process. What does the staff need to know and do? What do clients need to know and do?

Reinforcing how updated tools align with goals and outcomes also encourages tech adoption and usage. Connect changes back to the benefits users can expect to get throughout the end-user onboarding process.

## 10 Examples of Law Firm & Legal Digital Transformation

Modern law firms leverage emerging technologies and software solutions to automate repetitive tasks, secure data, improve efficiency, and nurture client relationships. Let’s explore ten of the most impactful and disruptive technologies transforming law firms across the legal sector to see what specific technologies are leading legal sector innovation.

### 1. Enterprise legal management software

Enterprise legal management (ELM) systems centralize capabilities from workflow automation to analytics under one umbrella. Access to real-time data supports continuous optimization helping all users stay on the same organizational page.

4DMedical previously relied on external counsel for legal advice and a team effort to manage its contracts registry using a spreadsheet, email, and shared drives. Its spreadsheets lacked the ability for a robust search functionality. If someone wanted to know what all contracts of a particular type looked like, what the budget was for them, or what insurance obligations were, the information wasn’t there.

The company used [LawVu](https://lawvu.com/customer-story/4d-medical/), an ELM system, to solve this problem. Having matter management and contract management in one unified legal system proved valuable by importing the team’s historic contracts into LawVu and using matters to group them into related data clusters. Not only are contracts now stored and easily searched for, but they are also organized into matters.

### 2. Generative AI

Generative AI has become a disrupter across industries and has caused major disruption in the legal sector, sparking excitement, fear, and reservations about implementing genAI-powered tools.

In [Wolter Kluwer’s Future Ready Lawyer](https://www.wolterskluwer.com/en/know/future-ready-lawyer-2023) survey of 700+ lawyers, 73% of respondents expect to integrate genAI into their legal workflows in the next 12 months, while 68% said they felt prepared for its impact. Another 73% said they understood how it could be applied to their work right now.

Implications include automation routing legal operational work and completing a large number of tasks in a small window of time. However, there are major risks as well, from AI hallucinations, biases, social implications, and a lack of understanding of how the technology is making decisions.

### 3. E-Notary software

Remote notarization software erases geographical barriers while upholding legal standards. Identity verification measures confirm signer authenticity for binding approvals applied digitally. This convenient option expands accessibility for clients while pre-populated templates ensure filings are formatted properly.

With [DocVerify](https://www.docverify.com/Products/E-Signatures/E-Signature-Applications), a leading e-notary software vendor, commissioned notary publics can create secure electronic notarizations that clients can access anytime, anywhere. This solution also enables instant electronic journal creation so notaries can streamline workflow and elevate their notary practice.

### 4. Conflict-checking solutions

Automated conflict screening cuts down on potential oversights. This works by completing comprehensive database checks before case commitments. Backed by AI, these tools detect obscure connections and can surface ethical concerns in seconds.

[CaseFox](https://www.casefox.com/conflict-check/)’s conflict-checking software allows users to enter the names of all the people involved in a case, on both sides. For example, when representing someone, you can enter their immediate family members. You can enter the names of people who have some kind of connection to the opposing party. The software will help you keep track of potential conflicts.

### 5. eDiscovery Platforms

eDiscovery solutions use data analysis to improve litigation. These platforms sort through document collections. Using AI, they can pinpoint legally significant details faster. Predictive analytics models also estimate case outcomes by comparing historical data, helping legal professionals make better decisions.

One joint defense group needed to find and understand the key players, timelines, and nuances involved in each litigation while preparing for hundreds of depositions, witness interviews, hearings, and trials scheduled across the litigation universe. However, traditional approaches to fact-finding and litigation (i.e., document review, keyword searches, etc.) were drowning case teams in extraneous and duplicative information.

[Lighthouse Global](https://www.lighthouseglobal.com/case-study/lighthouse-litigation-prep-proves-invaluable-in-complex-litigation) created a topic map across matters, which helped them quickly provide case teams with the core themes in each jurisdiction while reducing redundant search work. From there, as the case strategy for each matter developed, the solution team drilled down into more nuanced fact-finding to help surface the documents case teams needed to learn the key details of each matter.

### 6. Client portals

Modern [client portals](https://whatfix.com/blog/client-customer-portal-software/) strengthen relationships by providing [customer self-service capabilities](https://whatfix.com/blog/customer-self-service/). Convenience features like data access, document e-signatures, and messaging increase the transparency of interactions through self-service experiences for clients to access anytime through their web browser.

Client portal software from [MyCase](https://www.mycase.com/features/client-portal/) offers an all-in-one solution for communicating with clients. You can safely share and store important documents, emails, and messages with client portals. In addition, secure portals allow clients to easily share and document upcoming meetings, tasks, and events or even pay online.

### 7. Legal document drafting software

Automated drafting streamlines document creation by prompting users to locate approved templates without introducing human error risks. Staff can bring up agreements that the firm uses frequently in seconds.

Using automated legal templates built in [DocDraft](https://www.docdraft.ai/blogs/how-document-automation-software-for-lawyers-saved-time-money) streamlines document drafting processes, allowing legal professionals to focus on more critical tasks and provide better service to their clients. Quantifiable results show reduced hours spent on manual document creation and management, leading to increased productivity and more efficient use of resources.

### 8. Electronic filing platforms

E-filing platforms power digital submissions, condensing extensive paperwork into a few clicks. Legal teams benefit from the acceleration of approvals and court processing speeds these systems provide. The environmental perks of paper reduction also support firm sustainability initiatives.

[ABC Legal](https://www.abclegal.com/e-filing) makes it easy to upload and submit legal documents. Auto-fill forms save you precious time. We’ll check your filings to make sure they’re properly formatted and powerful filing tools get your documents through court portals fast. You can also track the status of any document you file.

### 9. Legal billing software

Billing management software centralizes invoice generation, time tracking, accounting, and revenue analytics. Cloud-based tools also support remote collaboration between attorneys and accounting staff to speed up payment collection.

Zahn Law Group attorneys couldn’t report the origination of expenses and the software they were using didn’t sync with other systems. With [LeanLaw](https://www.leanlaw.co/resource/case-study-billing-coordinator-dumps-harvest-for-leanlaw-saves-30-hours-month-with-real-time-continuous-qbo-sync/), origination is clear, time entries have vastly improved, and real-time data eases the stress of making timely and accurate payments.

### 10. Matter management software

Consolidating case management activities in comprehensive matter management systems connects everything related to the cases you work on. Collaborative functionality also empowers teams to work efficiently across matters.

The legal team at Healthscope manages a wide range of health patient, regulatory, commercial procurement, and employment legal issues. Its in-house legal department faced multiple challenges, including increased regulation, declining budgets, and increased demand for legal work.

Now, [Xakia](https://www.xakiatech.com/case-study/healthscope)‘s matter management system is the solution that provides the legal team with a single, central source of truth, providing improved visibility across work for the entire Healthscope Legal Department.

enriched_points.expanded_content: The legal industry is experiencing a significant shift, with technology integration changing traditional practices, improving workflows, and enhancing service delivery for law firms.

enriched_points.point_title: Importance of technology for law firms
enriched_points.expanded_content: Adopting technology is critical for law firms to remain competitive, streamline operations, and meet the evolving expectations of clients for efficient and accessible legal services.
Legal tech solutions promote operational efficiency by automating routine tasks, enabling faster response times, and ultimately improving client satisfaction through better service delivery.
Law firms face challenges in tech adoption, including resistance to change, integration with existing systems, and the need for staff training to ensure successful implementation.
